steps.title: Identify how Green and Extavour's approach differs
steps.content:
The passage describes Chan studying one genetic mechanism for convergence and Steiner studying a different one.

There's a knowledge gap: “the relative prevalence of convergence through shared and different genetic processes is still poorly understood.” Green and Extavour's innovation was to “evaluate both types of convergence in a single study”—studying both mechanisms together rather than separately.

steps.title: Match the difference to the choices
steps.content:
C states their study addresses convergent evolution “more comprehensively,” which is exactly right: they studied both mechanisms in one study, while Chan and Steiner had each studied only one.

The other choices either assume prior consensus the passage doesn't establish (A), mischaracterize the scope of Chan's research (B), or claim methodology the passage never mentions (D).

That matches choice C — C is correct.
